action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 4's SAKT model with cross-attention produced NaN values during validation. Root cause: the causal mask blocks position 0 from attending to any memory position (diagonal=0 means position t can only see 0..t-1, so position 0 sees nothing → softmax on all -inf → NaN). NaN propagates through training and corrupts model. Analysis: The cross-attention approach has a fundamental issue with position 0 having no valid memory. Instead, I should use self-attention with shifted corrects as input: input[t] = skill_emb(skill_ids[t]) + correct_emb(corrects[t-1]) + pos_emb(t). With diagonal=1 causal mask, position t sees 0..t, which includes its own skill but only previous corrects — no leakage, no NaN. Decision: Rewrite model with self-attention transformer, add proper NaN safeguards, train 30 epochs with cosine LR.
